title: lithocarpus auriculatus
intro: Lithocarpus auriculatus, also known as the Bur oak, is a deciduous tree species native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and rounded crown. The bark, leaves, and roots of the Lithocarpus auriculatus tree have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lithocarpus auriculatus include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating gastrointestinal problems, making it a valuable remedy in traditional Chinese medicine. Lithocarpus auriculatus can be prepared as tea, decoction, or infusion, and its extracts are also used in modern herbal supplements and pharmaceuticals.
